_meta.notes: Live data as of 2026-07-01, researched from official developer sites and local press. Critical fields (price, status, completion, deposit, HOA) should be re-verified with each developer sales gallery before quoting to clients. Renderings are used with attribution — every image carries an image_credit and must render with its courtesy line.
_meta.image_policy: All hero/card images are developer renderings used with 'Courtesy of' attribution via the image_credit field. Replace with owned photography as buildings complete.

projects.name: The Owen
projects.tagline: Twenty-nine boutique bayfront residences on Golden Gate Point — four per floor, delivering mid-2026.
projects.developer: The Ronto Group with Wheelock Street Capital
projects.residence_types: Four 3BR/3.5BA open-concept plans (two with study), plus four penthouses
projects.pet_policy: Up to two pets of any size (behavior restrictions apply)
amenities: Saltwater infinity-edge pool and spa
Landscaped amenity deck with fire pit, outdoor bar and grilling area
Club room, game room and board room
Fitness center with massage suite, yoga area and steam room
Fenced dog park and bocce court
Two secure covered parking spaces per residence (one EV pre-wired)
Semi-private elevator access
Balcony gas grills standard on every residence
projects.editorial_overview:
The Owen is the boutique play on Golden Gate Point — 29 residences in ten stories, four homes per floor, on a 1.18-acre bayfront site at the foot of the Ringling Bridge. If you want new construction without tower-scale density, this is the building I point clients to first. The developer is Naples-based Ronto Group, partnered with institutional investor Wheelock Street Capital — the same team behind Rosewood Residences Lido Key and the newly launched Amara two blocks away, which means an established local track record and real balance-sheet strength behind the delivery schedule.

Every floor plan is a three-bedroom, three-and-a-half-bath layout between 2,263 and 3,065 square feet under air, with semi-private elevator entry and a covered balcony that includes a built-in 32-inch gas grill — a detail almost no competing building offers. Kitchens run Sub-Zero, Wolf and Bosch with five-burner gas cooktops. The amenity level covers a saltwater infinity pool and spa, club and game rooms, a fitness center with steam and massage suites, work-from-home rooms, a fenced dog park and bocce.

The buyer fit: someone who wants a walk-to-downtown address (Main Street is minutes over the bridge approach) with the privacy and quiet of Golden Gate Point, at a price point — from the mid-$2 millions as of July 2026 — meaningfully below the Ritz-Carlton and Rosewood tier. Ground broke in June 2024, and delivery is Q2 2026, so this is one of the rare opportunities to buy essentially-new product without a two-year construction wait.

project_faqs.question: When will The Owen be completed?
project_faqs.answer: As of mid-2026, The Owen is in the final stretch of construction. The building broke ground in June 2024 and the developer lists delivery in the second quarter of 2026, with construction reported on schedule throughout — so first closings and move-ins are happening now. The sales gallery operates at 1819 Main Street in downtown Sarasota.

project_faqs.question: How much do residences at The Owen cost?
project_faqs.answer: As of mid-2026, The Owen's 29 residences are priced from the mid-$2 millions, varying by floor, plan and view. All four floor plans are three-bedroom, three-and-a-half-bath layouts of roughly 2,263 to 3,065 square feet under air, plus covered balconies with built-in gas grills. Two covered parking spaces are included with every residence.

projects.name: The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Sarasota Bay
projects.tagline: Seventy-eight flow-through bayfront residences at The Quay, with full Ritz-Carlton service — topped out and completing late 2026.
projects.residence_types: 3–4 BR flow-through waterfront residences and penthouses
amenities: Full Ritz-Carlton-managed services and concierge
Resort-style waterfront pool and amenity deck
Waterfront fitness and wellness center
Game room with racing and golf simulators
Kids' Club
Waterfront beverage bar
The Harbor Club — 20,000+ sq ft amenity campus (announced 2026)
Walkable access to The Quay's marina, dining and retail
projects.editorial_overview:
This is the flagship of the current downtown cycle: a 20-story tower at The Quay holding just 78 flow-through residences, each spanning the full depth of the building so you get Sarasota Bay on one exposure and the city skyline on the other. Developer Kolter Urban has delivered more than $10 billion in residential projects — including the original Ritz-Carlton Residences, Sarasota in 2020 — so this is their second Ritz-branded building in the city, not a first experiment. Architecture is by 10 Design with interiors by HBA Americas, and the brand's residential service operation runs the building day to day.

Residences run roughly 3,556 to more than 5,800 square feet in three- and four-bedroom layouts, priced from $3.7 million as of July 2026. The tower topped out in December 2025 and is tracking to a fourth-quarter 2026 completion. Demand has been unusually strong even by Quay standards — local business press reported roughly $25 million in contracts in a single 30-day window in early 2026, and Kolter has already announced a companion West Tower plus The Harbor Club, a 20,000-plus-square-foot amenity campus serving the community.

The buyer fit: someone who wants turnkey, brand-managed service — concierge, staffed amenities, hotel-grade maintenance culture — inside the Quay's walkable bayfront district, two blocks from the Van Wezel and the new Bay Park. It carries the strongest brand recognition of any project in this market, and resale liquidity in Ritz-branded Sarasota product has historically reflected that. Sales are handled exclusively by Premier Sotheby's International Realty; I can arrange gallery presentations and represent you independently of the developer's sales team.

project_faqs.question: When will The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Sarasota Bay be finished?
project_faqs.answer: As of mid-2026, the 20-story tower is under construction and topped out in December 2025, with developer Kolter Urban targeting completion in the fourth quarter of 2026. Interior build-out is underway. A newly announced West Tower and the 20,000+ sq ft Harbor Club amenity campus will follow on a later timeline, with pre-construction sales already active.

project_faqs.question: What do residences at The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Sarasota Bay cost?
project_faqs.answer: As of mid-2026, pricing begins around $3.7 million for the tower's 78 flow-through residences, which range from roughly 3,556 to more than 5,800 square feet in three- and four-bedroom layouts, topped by penthouses. Early 2026 saw about $25 million in contracts in a single month, so remaining inventory is limited and floor selection matters — buyers benefit from independent representation when negotiating premiums.

projects.name: Rosewood Residences Lido Key
projects.tagline: Sixty-five private beachfront residences on Lido Key — the first stand-alone residential project in Rosewood's history, welcoming owners in 2026.
projects.address: 1000 Benjamin Franklin Drive, Sarasota, FL 34236 (Lido Key)
projects.developer: The Ronto Group with Wheelock Street Capital; services by Rosewood Hotels & Resorts
projects.residence_types: 3–4 BR private beachfront residences, including penthouse-level homes
projects.hoa_fee_range: Reported ~$4,300–$6,250/mo by residence size — verify with sales gallery
amenities: Private beach club directly on Lido Beach
Two resort-style pools with daybeds and fire-pit terraces
Spa and wellness suite
Fitness center
Owners lounge and private dining venues
Theater and game/sports terrace
Guest suites for visitors
Rosewood-managed concierge and valet
projects.image_credit: Courtesy of The Ronto Group / Rosewood Residences Lido Key
projects.editorial_overview:
Rosewood Residences Lido Key is the single most significant thing to happen to Lido Key real estate in a generation: the first stand-alone, exclusively residential project in the 40-plus-year history of the Rosewood Hotels & Resorts brand — no hotel component, just 65 private residences directly on the sand. The Ronto Group and Wheelock Street Capital developed it; Miami high-rise specialist Swedroe Architecture designed the 11-story building; and New York's Lillian Wu Studio — the interior designer behind Rosewood properties in Bangkok and Washington, D.C. — handled interiors.

Residences run 3,335 to nearly 5,000 interior square feet in three- and four-bedroom layouts with floor-to-ceiling glass over the Gulf. The amenity program reads like a private club: a beach club on Lido's 3.5 miles of sand, two pools, a spa and wellness suite, a theater, guest suites, private dining and Rosewood-managed owner services throughout. Construction broke ground in January 2024, topped out in November 2024, and the building is welcoming owners through 2026, with reported sales past $239 million.

As of July 2026, remaining availability is priced from roughly $6.8 million to about $14 million, with reported monthly fees of approximately $4,300 to $6,250 depending on residence size. The buyer fit: someone who wants true beachfront — not bay, not near-beach — with hotel-brand service standards, a five-minute drive to St. Armands Circle and ten to downtown. It carries the entire ultra-luxury beachfront new-construction market on the Sarasota barrier islands right now, because nothing else like it is being built on Lido.

project_faqs.question: How much do residences at Rosewood Residences Lido Key cost?
project_faqs.answer: As of mid-2026, remaining beachfront residences range from roughly $6.8 million to about $14 million, spanning approximately 3,335 to 5,000 interior square feet in 3- and 4-bedroom layouts. Third-party sources report monthly fees of about $4,300 to $6,250 depending on residence size. With reported sales exceeding $239 million on 65 total residences, remaining inventory is limited — confirm current availability through the sales gallery or an independent buyer's agent.

project_faqs.question: What makes Rosewood Residences Lido Key unique in Sarasota?
project_faqs.answer: It is the first exclusively residential, stand-alone property ever developed under the Rosewood Hotels & Resorts brand — there is no hotel component. The 11-story building places 65 private residences directly on Lido Beach with Rosewood-managed services, a private beach club, spa, theater, guest suites and two pools. As of mid-2026 the building is completing construction and welcoming owners, following a January 2024 groundbreaking.

projects.name: Amara on Sarasota Bay
projects.tagline: Fifty-four residences in two bayfront towers on Golden Gate Point — the only new Sarasota project with private marina ownership.
projects.developer: The Ronto Group with Wheelock Street Capital
projects.residence_types: 2–4 BR residences across two 8-story towers; seven penthouses, two with private pools
projects.flood_zone: First residential level elevated 15 ft above grade
amenities: Private marina with owner-purchased boat slips (12–21 slips pending approval)
Bayfront infinity-edge pool with cabanas
Fitness center with sauna and steam room
Yoga lawn, bocce court and putting green
Waterfront esplanade along the shoreline
Social and gathering lounges
Penthouse-level terraces with bay views
projects.image_credit: Courtesy of The Ronto Group / Amara Sarasota
projects.editorial_overview:
Amara is the boater's answer on Golden Gate Point — and as of July 2026, the only new luxury development in Sarasota offering private marina ownership. Ronto Group and Wheelock Street Capital are building 54 residences across two eight-story towers on the former Pier 550 site along the east side of the Point, with a developer application in for 21 boat slips and a minimum of 12 anticipated for owner purchase, giving direct, unobstructed access to Sarasota Bay and the Gulf.

The Sarasota Planning Board unanimously approved the two-tower plan in January 2025. Each tower rises eight stories over podium parking with the first residential level elevated 15 feet above grade — a deliberate flood-resilience decision that also lifts every home's sightline. The design team pairs MHK Architecture with Lillian Wu Studio, the New York interior designer behind Rosewood Residences Lido Key. Residences run roughly 2,500 to over 3,700 square feet in two- to four-bedroom layouts, priced from about $4.2 million, with seven penthouses — two featuring private pools. Amenities center on the water: a bayfront infinity pool, waterfront esplanade, fitness with sauna and steam, yoga lawn, bocce and a putting green.

The site was cleared in 2025, groundbreaking was slated for early 2026, and construction is expected to take about two years — call it 2028 delivery. That timeline is the opportunity: this is the earliest-stage project on this page, which means the widest floor-plan selection and true pre-construction pricing. If a deeded slip outside your back door matters, nothing else in the market competes.

project_faqs.question: What makes Amara different from other new Sarasota condo projects?
project_faqs.answer: Amara is the only new luxury condominium in Sarasota offering private marina ownership. Owners on Golden Gate Point can purchase deeded boat slips at the on-site marina with direct, bridge-free access to Sarasota Bay and the Gulf. The developer has applied for 21 slips and anticipates at least 12 approved for owner purchase. Combined with just 54 residences across two 8-story towers, a bayfront infinity pool and waterfront esplanade, it targets boaters who also want downtown walkability.

project_faqs.question: How much do Amara condos cost and when will they be finished?
project_faqs.answer: As of mid-2026, pricing starts around $4.2 million, with penthouses reaching approximately $8.5 million. Residences span roughly 2,500 to over 3,700 square feet in two- to four-bedroom layouts. The site was cleared in 2025, groundbreaking was slated for early 2026, and construction is expected to take about two years — putting estimated completion around 2028. Sales run through Ronto Realty's gallery at 1819 Main Street.

projects.name: The Edge
projects.tagline: Twenty-seven oversized residences at Cocoanut and Fruitville — boutique scale, 10,000 sq ft of amenities, completing late 2026.
amenities: Fourth-floor 10,000 sq ft amenity level with pool
Cold plunge, spa and infrared sauna
Fitness center with yoga studio
Wine vaults and catering kitchen
Billiards and social lounge with outdoor balcony
Pet grooming area
Concierge services
projects.image_credit: Courtesy of The Edge Sarasota / Jebco Ventures
projects.editorial_overview:
The Edge is the local team's building: developed by Sarasota principal James Bridges of Jebco Ventures with ORE Development, designed by BDG Architects with interiors by DSDG's Mark Sultana, and built by Voeller Construction — a nearly all-Sarasota/Tampa roster backed by a $46 million construction loan. Ten stories, 27 residences, at the corner of Cocoanut Avenue and Fruitville Road, one block from the Rosemary District and a short walk to Main Street, the bayfront and The Bay park.

What sets it apart is residence size. Two- and three-bedroom plans run roughly 3,145 to 3,532 square feet — single-family scale in a boutique tower — with floor-to-ceiling glass throughout. The amenity program concentrates on a 10,000-square-foot fourth-floor deck: pool, cold plunge, spa, infrared sauna, fitness center with yoga studio, wine vaults, a catering kitchen and a billiards lounge with outdoor balcony.

The building topped out in December 2025 — buyers signed the final commemorative concrete pour — and is on schedule for late-2026 completion. As of July 2026, remaining residences are priced from $2.995 million, with earlier inventory having listed up to the mid-$5 millions, and only a limited number of homes remain. The buyer fit: someone who wants walk-everywhere downtown living with genuinely large rooms, at a price under the bayfront flagship towers, delivered on essentially the same timeline. Sales are handled through Michael Saunders & Company; I represent buyers independently.

project_faqs.question: When will The Edge Sarasota be completed?
project_faqs.answer: As of mid-2026, The Edge is under construction and on schedule for completion in late 2026. The 10-story building topped out in December 2025, and the official site advertises move-ins in late 2026. The project sits at 290 Cocoanut Avenue at Fruitville Road in downtown Sarasota, one block from the Rosemary District and a short walk to Main Street and The Bay park.

project_faqs.question: How much do condos at The Edge Sarasota cost?
project_faqs.answer: As of mid-2026, remaining two- and three-bedroom residences start at $2.995 million (2025 list prices ran roughly $3.3M to $5.5M, so remaining-inventory pricing has adjusted). The 27 residences measure approximately 3,145 to 3,532 square feet with floor-to-ceiling impact glass and a 10,000-square-foot amenity level. A limited number of homes remained available following the December 2025 topping-out.

projects.name: The Gallery
projects.tagline: Sixty European-inspired residences in the Rosemary District — downtown's rare new construction under $2M, occupancy from fall 2026.
projects.developer: Palm One Group with The Simpson Organization
projects.residence_types: 1–3 BR residences across six collections, incl. two-level open-staircase homes and a full-floor penthouse
projects.estimated_completion: Construction complete June 2026; occupancy from Sept 2026
projects.hoa_fee_range: $469–$1,491/mo (~$0.50 per sq ft), incl. water, sewer, trash, wind & building insurance
projects.short_term_rental_policy: 6-month minimum lease, max twice per year — owner-focused community
projects.flood_zone: Built above the floodplain — flood insurance not required
amenities: West-facing rooftop terrace pool with city and bay views
Rooftop social lounge
Two-room fitness center and yoga studio
Private elevator entries on many residences
Deeded private parking garage and storage
Private courtyards, garden terraces and rooftop terraces by collection
Open-air residence entries
projects.image_credit: Courtesy of Palm One Group / The Gallery Sarasota
projects.editorial_overview:
The Gallery occupies a niche almost nothing else in downtown Sarasota new construction touches: most of its 60 residences are priced under $2 million, and some start under $1 million — real new-construction pricing in a market where the marquee towers start at $3 million and climb. It's a six-level, European-inspired midrise at 4th Street and Cocoanut Avenue in the Rosemary District, three blocks from Sarasota Bay, developed by Palm One Group with The Simpson Organization and designed by Sarasota's own Halflants + Pichette.

The design organizes homes into six collections — private-courtyard residences, garden-terrace homes, two-level residences with open staircases and ceilings up to 26 feet, rooftop-terrace homes and a single full-floor penthouse of about 3,000 square feet. Entries are open-air rather than double-loaded corridors, which gives the building a residential, almost townhouse feel. The rooftop carries a west-facing pool and social lounge with city and bay views.

Two practical points I flag for every buyer here. First, fees: roughly $0.50 per square foot per month ($469–$1,491), including wind and building insurance — genuinely low for new downtown product, helped by post-tension concrete construction and a location above the floodplain that eliminates the flood-insurance line item entirely. Second, the leasing policy — six-month minimums, twice a year — keeps the building owner-occupied rather than investor-driven. As of July 2026, construction is finishing on schedule with certificates of occupancy expected from September; a bit over 20 residences remain, and some collections are down to a single home.

project_faqs.question: What do condos at The Gallery Sarasota cost, and what is still available?
project_faqs.answer: As of mid-2026, residences range from under $1 million to approximately $2 million, with one remaining full-floor penthouse of about 3,000 square feet offered at $3.5 million. Just over 20 of the 60 residences remain, spread across six collections — some are down to a single home. Monthly fees run roughly $469 to $1,491 (about $0.50 per square foot) and include water, sewer, trash, maintenance, and wind and building insurance.

project_faqs.question: When will The Gallery Sarasota be ready for move-in?
project_faqs.answer: As of mid-2026, construction is on schedule for June 2026 completion, with certificates of occupancy anticipated from September 2026. The building sits above the floodplain in the Rosemary District at 4th Street and Cocoanut Avenue, so flood insurance is not required — a notable advantage among downtown Sarasota new construction. Hardhat tours run monthly from the preview gallery at 625 S. Orange Avenue.

projects.name: Mira Mar Residences
projects.tagline: Seventy estate-scale residences in two 18-story towers rising behind the restored 1922 Mira Mar on Palm Avenue — delivery projected late 2028.
projects.residence_types: 3–4 BR estate residences in two towers; penthouses each include a guest cottage
amenities: 19th-floor rooftop Sky Lounge with Sarasota Bay views
70-foot lap pool with resort-style deck
Wellness spa with water-therapy pools, infrared sauna, steam and massage rooms
Fitness center and movement studio
Dog spa and open-air dog park
1922 Owners' Lounge and garden terrace in the restored historic building
Valet, concierge and luxury house car with driver
projects.image_credit: Courtesy of Seaward Development / Mira Mar Sarasota
projects.editorial_overview:
Mira Mar is the most architecturally consequential project downtown: two 18-story towers with 70 estate-scale residences rising behind the fully restored 1922 Mira Mar building — one of the oldest surviving structures from Sarasota's land-boom era, and the subject of a preservation fight that ended with the City Commission's unanimous May 2025 approval of a restore-plus-build plan after a 2022 demolition request was denied. The $29 million rehabilitation of the historic Mediterranean Revival building comes first; it will anchor shops and restaurants along Palm Avenue when complete.

The developer is Seaward Development's Patrick DiPinto, whose 18-story Epoch a few blocks south set the modern benchmark for Palm Avenue luxury. Nichols Architects designed the towers with REG Architects handling historic preservation and Suffolk Construction building. Residences are three- and four-bedroom layouts of roughly 3,300 to 6,000 square feet, priced from the high $3 millions as of July 2026 — and every penthouse includes its own separate guest cottage, a first for this market. Amenities span a 19th-floor Sky Lounge, 70-foot lap pool, wellness spa with water-therapy pools, a movement studio, dog spa and park, valet, concierge and a house car with driver.

The City issued the construction permit in April 2026 and work is underway, with completion projected for late 2028. The buyer fit: someone planning ahead who wants Palm Avenue — downtown's gallery-and-dining street, a block off the bayfront — at true pre-construction pricing with the longest runway for floor-plan selection of any tower project in the core.

project_faqs.question: What is happening with the historic Mira Mar building on Palm Avenue?
project_faqs.answer: The 1922 Mira Mar — a Mediterranean Revival landmark from Sarasota's land-boom era — is being fully restored rather than demolished. After a 2022 demolition request was denied, Seaward Development acquired the property in 2023 and won unanimous city approval in May 2025 for a preservation-plus-towers plan. As of mid-2026 the construction permit has been issued, rehabilitation of the historic structure is the first phase, and the restored building will house shops and restaurants along Palm Avenue.

project_faqs.question: How much do Mira Mar Residences cost and when will they be completed?
project_faqs.answer: As of mid-2026, Mira Mar Residences are priced from the high $3 millions, with 70 three- and four-bedroom condominiums of roughly 3,300 to 6,000 square feet across two 18-story towers; each penthouse includes a separate guest cottage. Construction is underway following the April 2026 permit, with completion projected for late 2028. Reservations are being accepted through the sales gallery at 1258 N. Palm Avenue.

projects.name: SIX88
projects.tagline: Ten residences, ten stories, a private marina on Sarasota Bay — Golden Gate Point's award-winning boutique tower, occupancy 2026.
projects.residence_types: Half-floor Signature residences, full-floor 360° residences, and a two-level penthouse with rooftop terrace
amenities: Private marina with boat slips on 80 ft of Sarasota Bay shoreline
Marina lounge with paddleboard and kayak launch
Jetted swim-spa pool with current system
Rooftop terrace with panoramic bay and skyline views
Private SIX88 Social Club
Fitness center
Oversized private garages for 2–4 vehicles
Dog grooming room
projects.image_credit: Courtesy of Palm One Group / SIX88 Sarasota
projects.editorial_overview:
SIX88 is the rarest format in this market: ten residences in ten stories on 80 feet of direct Sarasota Bay shoreline at the tip of Golden Gate Point. Floors four through seven are full-floor '360°' residences — your elevator opens into a home with water on every exposure — topped by a two-level penthouse with private rooftop terrace. The sculptural form, inspired by the curve of a yacht's sail, won the Platinum Award in SRQ Magazine's 2025 4WALLS Visionary Building Design Competition.

The team is the same Sarasota group behind The Gallery: developer Palm One Group, architect DSDG's Mark Sultana, and Voeller Construction. What SIX88 offers that virtually no boutique building can: a private marina with owner boat slips and a kayak/paddleboard launch, oversized private garages for two to four vehicles, a whole-building generator, a jetted swim-spa pool and a private social club. Interiors run Italian flat-panel cabinetry and panel-ready Thermador gas appliances, with a concierge design service for buyer customization.

As of July 2026, the building is in its final stage of construction with occupancy during 2026. Pricing starts around $2.48 million for the half-floor Signature residences, full-floor homes from $6.88 million, and the penthouse at $11.88 million. With ten homes total and sales already underway through Engel & Völkers Suncoast, availability is measured in single units — if the full-floor format on the bay is what you want, this is a call-me-this-week building, not a watch-the-market building.

project_faqs.question: How much do residences at SIX88 Sarasota cost?
project_faqs.answer: As of mid-2026, pricing begins around $2.48 million for lower-level half-floor Signature residences, while full-floor 360° residences on floors four through seven start at $6.88 million. The two-level penthouse is priced at $11.88 million. Residences span roughly 1,990 to 4,618 square feet, not counting terraces. With only ten homes total, availability is extremely limited.

project_faqs.question: What makes SIX88 different from other Golden Gate Point condos?
project_faqs.answer: SIX88 pairs boutique scale — ten residences in ten stories — with direct bayfront amenities rarely found at this size: a private marina with boat slips on 80 feet of shoreline, kayak and paddleboard launch, jetted swim-spa pool, rooftop terrace and private social club. Its yacht-sail-inspired design by DSDG Architects won SRQ Magazine's 2025 4WALLS Platinum Award. As of mid-2026 the building is in final construction with occupancy expected during 2026.
